quotes.text: Thou hast made us for thyself, O Lord, and our heart is restless until it finds its rest in thee.
quotes.author: Augustine of Hippo

quotes.text: Faith is to believe what you do not see; the reward of this faith is to see what you believe.
quotes.author: Augustine of Hippo

quotes.text: The measure of love is to love without measure.
quotes.author: Augustine of Hippo

quotes.text: To one who has faith, no explanation is necessary. To one without faith, no explanation is possible.
quotes.author: Thomas Aquinas

quotes.text: The things that we love tell us what we are.
quotes.author: Thomas Aquinas

quotes.text: Grace does not destroy nature but perfects it.
quotes.author: Thomas Aquinas

quotes.text: I believe in Christianity as I believe that the sun has risen: not only because I see it, but because by it I see everything else.
quotes.author: C.S. Lewis

quotes.text: You can never get a cup of tea large enough or a book long enough to suit me.
quotes.author: C.S. Lewis

quotes.text: Hardships often prepare ordinary people for an extraordinary destiny.
quotes.author: C.S. Lewis

quotes.text: Humility is not thinking less of yourself, it is thinking of yourself less.
quotes.author: C.S. Lewis

quotes.text: God whispers to us in our pleasures, speaks in our conscience, but shouts in our pains: it is his megaphone to rouse a deaf world.
quotes.author: C.S. Lewis

quotes.text: Cheap grace is the deadly enemy of our Church. We are fighting today for costly grace.
quotes.author: Dietrich Bonhoeffer

quotes.text: When Christ calls a man, he bids him come and die.
quotes.author: Dietrich Bonhoeffer

quotes.text: The test of the morality of a society is what it does for its children.
quotes.author: Dietrich Bonhoeffer

quotes.text: We pray for the big things and forget to give thanks for the ordinary, small (and yet really not small) gifts.
quotes.author: Dietrich Bonhoeffer

quotes.text: The function of prayer is not to influence God, but rather to change the nature of the one who prays.
quotes.author: Soren Kierkegaard
quotes.source: Purity of Heart Is to Will One Thing

quotes.text: Life can only be understood backwards; but it must be lived forwards.
quotes.author: Soren Kierkegaard

quotes.text: The tyrant dies and his rule is over; the martyr dies and his rule begins.
quotes.author: Soren Kierkegaard

quotes.text: There is a God-shaped vacuum in the heart of each man which cannot be satisfied by any created thing but only by God the Creator.
quotes.author: Blaise Pascal

quotes.text: The heart has its reasons which reason knows nothing of.
quotes.author: Blaise Pascal

quotes.text: All of humanity's problems stem from man's inability to sit quietly in a room alone.
quotes.author: Blaise Pascal

quotes.text: The Christian ideal has not been tried and found wanting; it has been found difficult and left untried.
quotes.author: G.K. Chesterton

quotes.text: Joy, which was the small publicity of the pagan, is the gigantic secret of the Christian.
quotes.author: G.K. Chesterton

quotes.text: The riddles of God are more satisfying than the solutions of man.
quotes.author: G.K. Chesterton
quotes.source: Introduction to the Book of Job

quotes.text: A Bible that is falling apart usually belongs to someone who is not.
quotes.author: Charles Spurgeon

quotes.text: God is too good to be unkind and He is too wise to be mistaken. And when we cannot trace His hand, we must trust His heart.
quotes.author: Charles Spurgeon

quotes.text: Pray and let God worry.
quotes.author: Martin Luther

quotes.text: We are saved by faith alone, but the faith that saves is never alone.
quotes.author: Martin Luther

quotes.text: Even if I knew that tomorrow the world would go to pieces, I would still plant my apple tree.
quotes.author: Martin Luther

quotes.text: There is not one blade of grass, there is no color in this world that is not intended to make us rejoice.
quotes.author: John Calvin

quotes.text: Man's nature, so to speak, is a perpetual factory of idols.
quotes.author: John Calvin

quotes.text: We can do nothing unless divine strength is given us from above; but we must act as if everything depended on us alone.
quotes.author: John Calvin

quotes.text: Let nothing disturb you, let nothing frighten you. All things are passing away: God never changes. Patience obtains all things. Whoever has God lacks nothing; God alone suffices.
quotes.author: Teresa of Avila

quotes.text: Christ has no body now but yours. No hands, no feet on earth but yours.
quotes.author: Teresa of Avila

quotes.text: All shall be well, and all shall be well, and all manner of thing shall be well.
quotes.author: Julian of Norwich

quotes.text: The greatest honor we can give Almighty God is to live gladly because of the knowledge of his love.
quotes.author: Julian of Norwich

quotes.text: He did not say: You will not be troubled, you will not be belabored, you will not be disquieted; but he said: You will not be overcome.
quotes.author: Julian of Norwich

quotes.text: What comes into our minds when we think about God is the most important thing about us.
quotes.author: A.W. Tozer

quotes.text: God never hurries. There are no deadlines against which he must work. Only to know this is to quiet our spirits and relax our nerves.
quotes.author: A.W. Tozer

quotes.text: The Christian does not think God will love us because we are good, but that God will make us good because He loves us.
quotes.author: C.S. Lewis

quotes.text: God loves each of us as if there were only one of us.
quotes.author: Augustine of Hippo

quotes.text: He is no fool who gives what he cannot keep to gain that which he cannot lose.
quotes.author: Jim Elliot

quotes.text: If you want to judge how well a person understands Christianity, find out how much he makes of the thought of being God's child, and having God as his Father.
quotes.author: J.I. Packer

quotes.text: God created us for this: to live our lives in a way that makes him look more like the greatness and the beauty and the infinite worth that he really is.
quotes.author: John Piper

quotes.text: Darkness is my closest friend.
quotes.author: Sons of Korah

quotes.text: True prayer is neither a mere mental exercise nor a vocal performance. It is far deeper than that. It is spiritual transaction with the Creator of heaven and earth.
quotes.author: Charles Spurgeon

quotes.text: God does not love us because we are valuable. We are valuable because God loves us.
quotes.author: Fulton J. Sheen

quotes.text: Joy is the infallible sign of the presence of God.
quotes.author: Pierre Teilhard de Chardin

quotes.text: The soul hardly ever realizes it, but whether he is a believer or not, his loneliness is really a homesickness for God.
quotes.author: Hubert van Zeller
